pages.raw_text:
Mrs. John Valance was among
the charming hostesses of yester-
day when she entertained compli-
mentary to Mrs. Lulu Loveland
Shepard of Salt Lake City.


The rooms of the Valance home
were beautifully decorated in a
color note of yellow and white, vel
low being the suffragist emblem,
while the white represented the w.
G. T. U. Potted palms and ferns
were banked about the rooms and
in the windows, while from the
chandeliers were hung yellow and
white crepe paper and floral de-
signs of carnations and buttercups.


Yellow and white flowers formed
a centerpiece at the table. The
places were marked with tiny
chicks.


During the afternoon Mrs. Shep.
ard gave an interesting talk and
she and Mrs. May Arkwright Hut
ton told of interesting expert
ences in their work as leaders OF
woman's suffrage and w. c. T. U
work.


Covers were laid for 25 guests.


4 4 e


BRIDGE LUNCHEON TODAY.


Mrs. J. Stanley Webster and
Miss Maude Beckon are entertain
ing at delightful affair today al
the home of the latter on Summit
boulevard.


A luncheon and afternoon al
z bridge will afford the amusement
of the guests, who will number
about so.


81 4 4 4


MISS RICHARDSON is HOSTESS


Miss May Richardson enter
tanned the members of the Sigma
= Chi Chi Sewing club yesterday a,
g her home on Lincoln street.


The affair was informal, about
nine guests being present. The
hours We're spent with their sew
ing, music and with conversation.
